other_observers: none; I was alone when I observed the bird.
specific_location: along the levee embankment at Meadow Creek Reservoir, along County Road 30 east of CO 14 near North Park.
description: Small (killdeer-sized), with gray wings, white underparts, a noticeably yellow bill, and a black cap with a white forehead mark that at a distance can look like a partial eyebrow stripe.Behaviors: I came across the bird as I began to drive onto the reservoir. At a distance of a few hundred feet, I got my scope out and IDed it. It was sitting on the road, facing into the wind which was coming from the NW or WNW. I drove a little closer, and photographed it. It did not move. I drove even closer, and carefully approached it, but eventually it flushed. It flew out over the water, and then angled northward, flying with some gulls away from the reservoir. It did not return in the short time that I remained in the area.Call: nonePlumage: definitive alternate
photographer: Photographer: Eric DeFonso, Eric DeFonso, Eric DeFonso, Eric DeFonso
